## Description

<!-- What does this PR do? Why is it needed? Link any related issues. -->

Closes #

---

## Type of Change

- [ ] `fix` — bug fix (does not break existing functionality)
- [ ] `feat` — new feature (backwards-compatible)
- [ ] `feat!` — breaking change (removes or changes existing API)
- [ ] `refactor` — internal restructure, no behaviour change
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `test` — add or update tests
- [ ] `build` — build system, dependencies, tooling
- [ ] `chore` — housekeeping (CI config, .gitignore, etc.)

---

## Testing Done

<!-- Describe how you tested this change. -->

- [ ] `yarn typecheck` passes
- [ ] `yarn lint` passes
- [ ] `yarn test` passes
- [ ] Manually tested on Android (if relevant)
- [ ] Manually tested on iOS (if relevant)
- [ ] Manually tested on Web (if relevant)

---

## Checklist

- [ ] My commit message follows [Conventional Commits](../CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-message-convention)
- [ ] I have signed my commits with `Signed-off-by: Name <email>` (DCO — see [DCO](../DCO))
- [ ] Tests are added or updated for the change (where applicable)
- [ ] Documentation is updated (README, inline comments) if the public API changed
- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
